title: Enhance Verilator to include compile and runtime statistics in its output at the End
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

**What would you like added/supported?**

Enhance Verilator to include compile and runtime statistics in its output:

- Compilation Time: Report the CPU time taken to compile the Verilog/SystemVerilog source code.
- Elaboration Time: Report the CPU time taken to elaborate the design and generate the circuit model.
- Link Time (if applicable): Report the time taken to link the design with any external libraries or components.
- Simulation Time: Report the CPU time taken to simulate the design.


This information would provide valuable insights into the performance of different hardware configurations and designs, without the need for additional debugging flags. It would be displayed as 5 additional lines in the terminal output or logs, providing a concise summary of the compile and runtime statistics.

**Can you attach an example that runs on other simulators?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)**

```
           V e r i l a t o r   S i m u l a t i o n   R e p o r t 

<Verilator Version information>
CPU Time
Compilation Time:   9.935 seconds
Elaboration Time:   0.288 seconds
Linking Time:       0.591 seconds 
Simulation Time:    0.600 seconds

Tue Feb 20 02:08:18 2024 <Date and Time>
```
We may provide the information about the number of processor `proc` used if you feel it would be helpful.

The above report may change for compilation in which we may exclude the Simulation time

**May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?**
No, I don't C/C++
